data.title: Lloyds Banking Group Buys Back and Cancels 10 Million Shares
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

An announcement from Lloyds Banking ( (GB:LLOY) ) is now available.

Lloyds Banking Group has repurchased 10 million of its ordinary shares on 30 January 2026 as part of its existing share buyback programme, paying a volume-weighted average price of 108.1041 pence per share. The bank intends to cancel the repurchased shares, a move that will reduce the overall share count and can enhance earnings per share and capital return metrics for investors, underlining ongoing efforts to manage its capital structure and return surplus capital to shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:LLOY) stock is a Hold with a £1.06 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lloyds Banking stock, see the GB:LLOY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GB:LLOY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GB:LLOY is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weaker financial quality signals—especially negative free cash flow and higher leverage—despite supportive technical strength and a clearly positive earnings-call outlook with upgraded 2026 targets and increased capital returns. Valuation is a mild headwind given the P/E relative to the dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on GB:LLOY stock, click here.

More about Lloyds Banking

Lloyds Banking Group is one of the UK’s largest banking groups, providing retail and commercial banking, lending, savings and other financial services. It focuses primarily on the UK market through well-known high street and digital brands, serving individual consumers, small and medium-sized enterprises, and larger corporate clients.

Average Trading Volume: 149,133,775

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fulcrum Metals Plc ( (GB:FMET) ) has provided an update.

Fulcrum Metals plc has confirmed that its issued ordinary share capital now consists of 138,287,039 ordinary shares of 1 pence each, all carrying one voting right, with no shares held in treasury, making the total number of shares and voting rights in the company 138,287,039. This updated share capital figure provides the reference point shareholders must use to determine whether they are required to disclose any holdings or changes in holdings under the UK Financial Conduct Authority’s Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, ensuring clarity and transparency around the company’s ownership structure.

More about Fulcrum Metals Plc

Fulcrum Metals plc is an AIM-listed, technology-led natural resources company focused on recovering precious and critical metals from mine tailings in Canada using environmentally friendly leaching technology licensed from Extrakt Process Solutions. Its initial projects target tailings from the historic Teck-Hughes and Sylvanite gold mines in the Kirkland Lake mining camp in Ontario, and it also holds a portfolio of mineral exploration and development assets across Ontario and Saskatchewan, positioning it to become a near-term, environmentally conscious gold producer in two of Canada’s largest historic gold camps.

data.title: Nickel North Launches $3 Million Private Placement to Advance Quebec Critical Minerals Project
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Nickel North Exploration ( (TSE:NNX) ) is now available.

Nickel North Exploration Corp. has announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 60 million units at $0.05 per unit, for gross proceeds of up to $3 million, with each unit consisting of one common share and one-half warrant exercisable at $0.08 for two years. The financing, which includes insider participation and remains subject to TSX Venture Exchange and other regulatory approvals, will fund field exploration at the company’s Hawk Ridge Ni-Cu-PGE-Co project in Northern Quebec, general working capital and potential asset acquisitions, underscoring Nickel North’s efforts to advance its critical minerals portfolio in a strategically important jurisdiction for battery and energy-transition metals.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:NNX) stock is a Sell with a C$0.05 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Nickel North Exploration stock, see the TSE:NNX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TSE:NNX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:NNX is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by very weak financial performance (pre-revenue, ongoing losses, negative free cash flow, and deeply negative equity with debt). Technicals are a partial offset with strong trend indicators, but overbought signals increase downside risk. Valuation remains unattractive/unclear due to negative earnings and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:NNX stock, click here.

More about Nickel North Exploration

Nickel North Exploration Corp. is a Canada-based mineral exploration company focused on defining a copper-nickel-cobalt-platinum group element resource at its Hawk Ridge Project in Northern Quebec. The property comprises a 50 km-long belt of magmatic Cu-Ni-Co-PGE occurrences over 179.67 km² in a mining-friendly jurisdiction, and the company has been recognized by Quebec’s Ministry of Economics and Innovation as a key player in the province’s critical and strategic minerals value chain, while emphasizing sustainable development and strong relationships with local Inuit communities.

data.title: Takeda Advances TAK-279 in Ulcerative Colitis: What Investors Should Watch Next
data.description:
Takeda Pharmaceutical Company (TAK) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Study Overview: Takeda is running a Phase 2 study called “A Phase 2, Multicenter, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blind Induction Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Oral TAK-279 in Subjects With Moderately to Severely Active Ulcerative Colitis.” The goal is to see if TAK-279 can safely reduce bowel inflammation and symptoms in people with moderate to severe ulcerative colitis, a chronic bowel disease with high unmet need. The study also tracks side effects and how well patients tolerate treatment, which will guide whether Takeda moves this drug into later-stage trials and possible commercialization.

Intervention/Treatment: The study tests TAK-279 (also known as zasocitinib), an oral drug in capsule form, against placebo. Participants receive one of two doses of TAK-279 or a matching placebo for 12 weeks, then everyone moves onto active TAK-279 for up to 52 weeks. The aim is to offer a convenient, once-oral option that can reduce inflammation and improve day-to-day symptoms for ulcerative colitis patients.

Study Design: This is an interventional trial where participants are randomly assigned to different groups, similar to drawing lots. The model is “parallel,” meaning each group receives its assigned treatment at the same time. The trial is “double-blind” with four levels of blinding: patients, doctors, study staff, and outcome assessors do not know who gets the drug or placebo during the first 12 weeks. The main goal is treatment-focused: to see if TAK-279 works better than placebo while remaining safe.

Study Timeline: The study was first submitted on February 2, 2024, marking the formal start of regulatory review and site setup. The trial is currently recruiting, and participants will be treated for up to one year, with a 12-week induction phase and a 52-week treatment phase. The most recent update to the trial record was posted on January 29, 2026, signaling that the protocol and status have been reviewed and refreshed. The primary completion and final completion dates are still ahead, meaning key efficacy and long-term safety data are not yet available to the market.

Market Implications: For investors, this update underscores Takeda’s push to expand its inflammatory bowel disease portfolio with a new oral option. If TAK-279 shows strong efficacy and safety, it could enhance Takeda’s competitive position against existing biologics and newer oral agents in ulcerative colitis, potentially supporting medium- to long-term revenue growth and sentiment toward TAK shares. The trial’s rigorous randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind design should help generate data that regulators and payers trust, which is important when competing with established players in immunology. However, as a Phase 2 study, clinical and regulatory risk remain high, and any material stock impact is more likely when top-line results are announced rather than on procedural updates. The latest status confirms the program is active and progressing, a modest positive for pipeline visibility but not yet a clear catalyst. The study is ongoing and has been recently updated, with further details available on the ClinicalTrials.gov portal.

To learn more about TAK’s potential, visit the Takeda Pharmaceutical Company drug pipeline page.
data.author: TipRanks Clinical-Trials-Auto-Generated Newsdesk

data.title: Mega Fortune Delays Annual 20-F Yearly Report
data.description:
Mega Fortune Company Limited ( (MGRT) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mega Fortune Company Limited has notified investors that it will be late in filing its annual Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025, via a Form 12b-25. The delay relates specifically to its Form 20-F (Yearly Report), which will contain the audited financial statements for the year.

The company attributes the holdup to administrative delays in preparing the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) and, in particular, to a delay in obtaining the necessary consent from a prior auditor whose work must be included in the filing. There is no indication of internal control failures, a change of auditor, or a special committee review; the issue is presented as procedural and administrative rather than operational or financial.

Mega Fortune expects to file the delayed Form 20-F (Yearly Report) on or before the 15th calendar day after the original due date, in line with the relief provided under Form 12b-25. The company also confirms that all other required SEC periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, signaling an otherwise consistent compliance record.

Importantly for investors, the company states that it does not anticipate any significant change in its operating results compared with the prior fiscal year to be reflected in the upcoming earnings statements. However, the notification includes standard forward-looking statement language, cautioning that actual results and timing could differ due to various risks and uncertainties, including potential changes to anticipated financial results and the timing of auditor consent.

The filing underscores Mega Fortune’s intention to remain in compliance with SEC reporting requirements, using the permitted extension to finalize administrative matters. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer Siu Fung Tang, dated January 30, 2026, signaling executive-level oversight of the delayed filing and the ongoing effort to complete the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) promptly.

More about Mega Fortune Company Limited

Average Trading Volume: 137,815

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $86.63M
